content:
✅ **All 3 prediction papers for Design of Smart Cities (BCSE316L) have been created successfully.**

### Files Created:

1. **`D:\Study Papers\Joel\Design_of_Smart_Cities\Predictions\Predicted_CAT1_2026.md`**
   - Format: 5 × 10 = 50 marks, 90 minutes
   - Topics: Smart city definition & pillars, ICT in urban planning, WSN architecture, IoT for infrastructure, Singapore vs Dubai comparison
   - Includes Fill-in-the-blank + True/False (Q1b) matching the exact 2025 format

2. **`D:\Study Papers\Joel\Design_of_Smart_Cities\Predictions\Predicted_CAT2_2026.md`**
   - Format: 5 × 10 = 50 marks, 90 minutes
   - Topics: Smart energy grids, smart transportation (ITS), smart water management system design, urban data analytics, smart waste management
   - Higher Bloom's levels (L4–L5) as typical of CAT-2 papers
   - Includes a system design question (L5) for water management

3. **`D:\Study Papers\Joel\Design_of_Smart_Cities\Predictions\Predicted_FAT_2026.md`**
   - Format: 10 × 10 = 100 marks, 3 hours
   - Full syllabus coverage across all modules
   - Includes 2 numerical/calculation questions (energy grid + data storage)
   - 3 design-level (L5) questions with architecture diagrams expected
   - Comprehensive Barcelona/Singapore/Dubai case study comparison as final question
   - Cybersecurity + DPDPA 2023 coverage
   - Citizen participation platforms + campus-based design question

### Common Features Across All Papers:
- VIT Chennai header with course code, programme, semester, time, marks
- Table format with Q.No / Question / M / CO / BL columns
- Q1 always split into conceptual (5M) + objective fill-in-blanks/T-F (5M)
- Prediction confidence tables with percentage ratings and reasoning
- Preparation tips and diagram checklists at the bottom
